posts.title: This MCP Server Could Have Been a JSON File
posts.subtitle: There's a lot of buzz around MCP. I'm not convinced it needs to exist.

posts.title: AI IDEs Need Moats
posts.subtitle: VS Code eliminated the switching cost for AI IDEs. They need to build moats to survive. Partnering with software vendors and new open source projects could help.

posts.title: These Aren’t the Catalogs You’re Looking For
posts.subtitle: Metadata is useful for data warehouses, streaming systems, humans, and machines. We don't need different catalogs for each use case, though. Catalog convergence is here.

posts.title: Epsio, IVMs, and Differential Dataflow With Gilad Kleinman
posts.subtitle: Gilad Kleinman, CEO of Epsio, talks with me about incremental view maintenance (IVM). We discuss use cases, the history of IVM, how Epsio is different, and where the field is headed.

posts.title: Infrastructure Vendors Are in a Tough Spot
posts.subtitle: Cloud native, AI-enabled, post-ZIRP companies are the new apex predator.

posts.title: S3 Is the New SFTP
posts.subtitle: Customers want their data. A customer data lake is a great way to give it to them.

posts.title: The Quest for a Distributed POSIX-Compatible Filesystem
posts.subtitle: Distributed POSIX filesystems have proven elusive, but we're getting closer. Perhaps that's all we need.

posts.title: It's Time to Merge Analytics and Data Engineering (Again)
posts.subtitle: Data pipelines are commoditized and analytics engineers don't provide enough value.

posts.title: DuckDB Is Not a Data Warehouse
posts.subtitle: DuckDB is a tool, not a product.

posts.title: Small Batch, Artisanal ETL is Back

posts.title: The New Era of Flexible Infrastructure Deployment
posts.subtitle: Flexible deployment is now table stakes. Infrastructure must run embedded, client-side, single-node, clustered, as SaaS, BYOC, and self-hosted.

posts.subtitle: JP, the creator of FizzBee, talks about formal methods, TLA+, distributed systems verification, and a better way forward.

posts.title: Modular Monoliths Are a Good Idea, Actually
posts.subtitle: Microservices aren't the only way to get high cohesion and low coupling.

posts.subtitle: Philippe Noël is CEO and co-founder of ParadeDB. In this post, Philippe and I discuss ParadeDB, the experience of building as a PostgreSQL extension, pg_duckdb, pg_lakehouse, and more ...

posts.title: Unpacking the Buzz around ClickHouse
posts.subtitle: A look at the excitement around ClickHouse. I break down what makes it great and the challenges ahead.

posts.subtitle: Let's all agree on Iceberg's REST API and let the query engines dictate its evolution. Vendors can compete on enterprise features.

posts.subtitle: Why Samza failed, how it led to Kafka Streams and Kafka Connect, and why I'm skeptical of Apache Flink.

posts.title: Databases Should Speak Substrait
posts.subtitle: It's silly to have applications generate text-based SQL; they should be allowed to pass query plans to the database.

posts.subtitle: Everything you need to know about SQLite's type system; Shower thoughts on durable execution, workflows, and their user interfaces; and HikariCP is a giant performance flex.

posts.subtitle: Stream processors and outboxes don't fix change data capture. Engineers need a tool that versions streams, detects changes, and helps manage schema evolution.

posts.subtitle: There's a new protocol to save streaming; 1brc elevates some cool terminal benchmarking tools; and I compare Zed to Recap.

posts.subtitle: Viewstamped Replication makes a comeback; I dig into distributed simulation testing with Dropbox, FoundationDB, and Tigerbeetle.
